title: HSDS-FX conformance vector manifest
description: One manifest per normative wire AREA (design §8/§7/§6.2-6.3/§9). Pure, language-agnostic data: a foreign implementation in any language runs these vectors through the adapter contract (conformance/hsdsfx/README.md) with zero reference to PPR code. Expected values are byte-as-hex or canonical-base64 strings, never language-native types.

area.description: The normative wire element this manifest certifies (e.g. envelope_content_address).

spec.description: The HSDS-FX spec section this area maps to (e.g. HSDS-FX/§8.1).

reference_impl.description: file:symbol of PPR's reference implementation of this area (informational; an adapter need not use it).

interop_status.description: anchored = the expected bytes derive from a vendored upstream conformance suite (not self-graded). interop_pending = a PPR-native canonical reading (the new HSDS-FX composition) that only a second independent implementation can finally settle (see vendor/INTEROP_PENDING.md).

derives_from.description: For anchored areas: the vendored suite the expected bytes come from (e.g. vendor/jcs_rfc8785). For interop_pending: the INTEROP_PENDING.md row reference.

id.description: Stable vector id, unique within the area (e.g. env-id-001).

input.description: The vector input — an object/array/scalar the adapter operation consumes. Crypto seeds/keys are hex strings so any-language impls bind them.

expected.description: The required output for an accept vector — byte-as-hex / canonical-base64 / a JSON shape. ABSENT iff must_reject is true.

must_reject.description: true: a conforming impl MUST reject this input (raise / return false / 4xx); there is no `expected`. false: the impl MUST produce `expected` byte-for-byte.

interop_pending.description: Per-vector override of the area default; true marks a value pinned-by-fiat awaiting cross-impl confirmation.

interop_row.description: Pointer into vendor/INTEROP_PENDING.md for an interop_pending value.

allOf.comment: An accept vector (must_reject=false) MUST carry expected; a reject vector MUST NOT.
